claimText: The Environmental Accounting on Forest 2025 Report was released by the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI).
evidenceExcerpt: Environmental Accounting on Forest 2025 Report released by MoSPI is the first dedicated publication on forest accounting
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the publishing ministry of environmental reports.
distractorHooks: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC)

claimText: The Environmental Accounting on Forest 2025 Report is based on the UN System of Environmental Economic Accounts (SEEA) Framework, which integrates economic and environmental data.
evidenceExcerpt: based on the UN System of Environmental Economic Accounts (SEEA) Framework. SEEA integrates economic and environmental data.
examUse: Statement-based question on the methodology and framework used for India's forest accounting.
distractorHooks: Framework that separates economic and environmental data

claimText: According to ISFR 2023, India's total forest and tree cover stands at 25.17 percent of its geographical area.
evidenceExcerpt: ISFR 2023 shows total forest and tree cover increased to 25.17 percent of geographical area (21.76 percent Forest cover, 3.41 percent Tree cover).
examUse: Statement-based question testing exact forest and tree cover statistics.
distractorHooks: 21.76 percent of geographical area (confusing total with only forest cover)
24.56 percent of geographical area (older ISFR data)

claimText: Chhattisgarh, Uttar Pradesh, Odisha, and Rajasthan recorded the maximum increase in forest cover according to ISFR 2023.
evidenceExcerpt: Maximum increase seen in Chhattisgarh, UP, Odisha, and Rajasthan.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question identifying states with the highest growth in forest cover.

claimText: Eight states and Union Territories in India, including Mizoram, Lakshadweep, A&N Islands, and Arunachal Pradesh, have a forest cover of more than 75 percent of their geographical area.
evidenceExcerpt: 8 states/UTs have forest cover above 75 percent, including Mizoram, Lakshadweep, A&N Islands, and Arunachal Pradesh.
examUse: Statement-based question on the distribution of highly dense forest cover across states and UTs.
distractorHooks: Only North Eastern states have forest cover above 75 percent
15 states/UTs have forest cover above 75 percent
Madhya Pradesh has forest cover above 75 percent
